title: Star Wars (2015) 44
content:
{{Top|rwm|aor|title=''Star Wars'' (2015) 44}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars 44}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:StarWars2015-44.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'' 44 |writer=[[Kieron Gillen]] |penciller=[[Salvador Larroca]] |inker=Salvador Larroca |letterer=[[Clayton Cowles]] |colorist=[[Guru-eFX]] |cover artist=*[[David Marquez]] *[[Matthew Wilson]] |editor=[[Jordan D. White]] |publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] |release date=[[March 7]], [[2018]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/66456/star_wars_2015_44|text=Star Wars (2015) #44}} |pages=23 |upc=759606081134 |timeline=[[1 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars Book X: Mutiny at Mon Cala]]'' to [[1 ABY]]. |series=[[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'' (2015)]] |issue=44 }} '''''Star Wars'' 44''' is the forty-fourth issue of the [[Star Wars (2015)|''Star Wars'' comic series]]. It was written by [[Kieron Gillen]], illustrated by [[Salvador Larroca]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[March 7]], [[2018]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''THE REBELLION NEEDS YOU! The Empire takes from all of us—our freedom, our dignity, our hopes and for some, even our lives. The Rebel Alliance fights to take back our galaxy from the forces of oppression…but we need your help! Be a part of the solution—JOIN THE REBELLION TODAY!'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Part I
MUTINY AT MON CALA |text=It is a period of rebuilding in the galaxy. The Death Star has been destroyed as has the Imperial orbital drill on Jedha. A spark of hope has emerged among the Rebellion. Recently, rebel leaders Princess Leia, Luke Skywalker, and Han Solo have been in search of a new base of operations as they continue the fight against the Galactic Empire's tyrannical reign. New alliances are formed every day....}} ==Plot summary== On a [[Mon Cala]] island, Leia, Han, Luke, and their droids meet with [[Grand Admiral]] [[Dors Urtya|Urtya]] of the Mon Cala Mercantile Fleet. Han, who had smuggled them into the restricted nature zone, is confident they can handle any trouble. However, an Imperial patrol ship fires on their location, and their ship is destroyed. As stormtroopers chase them through the woods, Leia continues to brief Urtya, explaining that with the [[Death Star]] gone, now is the time to disrupt the Empire. She tells him that the Rebellion needs the Mon Cala fleet, the greatest merchant fleet in the galaxy, and that they can be refitted into warships and hidden across the galaxy. Urtya struggles with the decision to put his people in danger, and the heroes ultimately leave without his commitment. As they pilot the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'' to a Rebel production center, a dejected Leia worries about the future of the Rebellion without the Mon Calamari fleet. With a sudden idea, she has C-3PO contact Queen Trios, asking her to grant them access to a high-security Imperial prison system to break out a king. ==Continuity== The starship design of the [[Unidentified Mon Calamari cruiser (Yavin's Hope)|''Yavin's Hope'' type Mon Calamari cruiser]] is visually identical to a fan-made design from [[2014]], by an artist with the username "Jonathan" on [[Wikipedia:SketchUp#3D Warehouse|3D Warehouse]]. The work is titled "Croiseur Mon Calamari MC90 — Forme 3,"{{WebCite|url=https://3dwarehouse.sketchup.com/model/a03901f2c9f34c1311e411e4a9728447/Croiseur-Mon-Calamari-MC90-Forme-3|text=Croiseur Mon Calamari MC90 – Forme 3|author=Jonathan|work=[[Wikipedia:SketchUp#3D Warehouse|3D Warehouse]]|archivedate=20220117032518}} while another starship design, the [[Unidentified three-pronged Mon Calamari cruiser|three-pronged Mon Calamari cruiser]], is also visually identical to another design by Jonathan on [https://3dwarehouse.sketchup.com/model/8ece60e661d403ea7d9b14b827884353/Croiseur-Mon-Calamari-MC90-Forme-2 3Dwarehouse.sketchup.com]. The work is titled as "Croiseur Mon Calamari MC90 — Forme 2."{{WebCite|url=https://3dwarehouse.sketchup.com/model/8ece60e661d403ea7d9b14b827884353/Croiseur-Mon-Calamari-MC90-Forme-2|text=Croiseur Mon Calamari MC90 - Forme 2|author=Jonathan|work=[[Wikipedia:SketchUp#3D Warehouse|3D Warehouse]]|archivedate=20171208232059}} The word "croiseur" is the [[Wikipedia:French language|French]] translation for "cruiser."{{WebCite|url=https://www.collinsdictionary.com/dictionary/french-english/croiseur|text=English Translation of “CROISEUR”|work=[[Wikipedia:Collins English Dictionary|''Collins English Dictionary'']]|archivedate=20121225095122}} ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars (2015) 44}} ===Editions=== *UPC 759606081134; [[March 7]], [[2018]]; [[Marvel Comics]] **04411;
